meta.setting: Jefferson State frontier, opening around French Gulch, Clear Creek, Shasta, and the Klamath mountains
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western: horseback travel, camp life, hunting, debt, law pressure, outlaw choice, and a hard 1899 frontier tone

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Clear Creek gold rush context, Wintu presence, mining communities, and later logging and ranching context.

research_grounding.title: NPS The Wintu
research_grounding.use: Wintu homeland around Clear Creek, seasonal movement, acorns, salmon, burning, and Gold Rush displacement.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Northern California mining commerce, brick ruins, courthouse memory, iron shutters, and road-town atmosphere.

research_grounding.title: Redding Rancheria
research_grounding.use: Living Pit River, Wintu, and Yana sovereignty, culture, families, and traditional homelands.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the opening world document for an 1899 Jefferson State RDR-clone western packet. Do not write the final page inside this JSON.
page_shape: H1 project title with a short tabletop genre phrase
square image reference immediately after H1
inscription-style blockquote that functions as a threshold marker; invent the wording during page generation
bold one-line identity for the project
second blockquote as arrival mark; invent the wording during page generation
four short blockquote marks with plain-language meanings generated later
generation_guidance: Define French Gulch as the first playable pressure point without resolving the whole frontier.
Frame Jefferson State as contested, lived-in, and half-recorded by road books, claim papers, oral memory, and courthouse habit.
Let Native families, miners, merchants, livestock men, law officers, outlaws, cooks, riders, and animals share the same difficult map.
Make the page useful for play: it should give tone, entry points, social pressure, and the promise of hard choices.
Keep the text invitational but dusty: more trail ledger than heroic manifesto.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: Let the introduction smell like wet pine, lamp oil, iron filings, beans, leather, and county paper left too near a stove. It should feel hand-folded into the front of a road book by someone who knows the road can punish fancy words.
generation_prompt.table_spark: The first page should feel like the moment a rider sees French Gulch smoke through rain and realizes every debt has a witness.
must_preserve: French Gulch is the first playable place.
The tone is grounded 1899 western, not medieval, not alternate-modern, not clean nostalgia.
Native communities are living peoples with land, families, rights, work, memory, and political presence.
The generated markdown must include the image reference path exactly: images/frontier-of-jefferson.jpg.

style_constraints.era: Everything generated from this prompt belongs to 1899: horses, ore wagons, soot-black lamps, telegraph where appropriate, hand tools, rifles, ledgers, canvas, iron, mud, pine, and courthouse paper.
style_constraints.rdr_clone: Aim at an RDR-clone western tabletop packet: moral pressure, travel friction, camp routines, hunting, law heat, debt, reputation, and open-world consequence without copying named characters, missions, towns, logos, or slogans.
style_constraints.voice: Use a weathered oral-history cadence with practical table utility. The page should sound like it came from people who have slept outside, hauled ore, read tracks, and argued over land.
style_constraints.native_representation: Native American and tribal material must be grounded, specific, and respectful: living families, trade, land rights, stewardship, survival, diplomacy, and conflict. Do not invent sacred rites, fake language, costumes, or mystical tropes.
style_constraints.shape_priority: Follow the requested section order and heading levels exactly while inventing fresh 1899 western language.

avoid: do not write finished page prose inside this prompt file
do not include completed lore paragraphs, sample quotations, dialogue, final move text, or final rule text
do not mention any source packet, template packet, reference project, or inherited taxonomy
do not use copied names or terms from any prior non-western packet
do not include engine, hardware, runtime, profiling, build, deployment, or implementation details
do not include modern slang, modern technology, theme-park nostalgia, fake tribal language, or sacred ceremony invention
do not use placeholder filler such as TBD, lorem ipsum, or generic western flavor without a playable purpose
self_check: The generated markdown follows the required heading order exactly.
This JSON contains guidance only and no finished page prose.
The generated page is recognizably 1899 Jefferson State and points back to French Gulch.
Native people, settlers, animals, lawmen, workers, and outlaws are represented as grounded social forces, not icons.
No copied terms, inherited non-western vocabulary, modern props, or reference/source project names appear.
